text.title: files-reference Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Returning slugs invented by the picker rather than copied verbatim from a catalog section.
antipattern.symptom: Downstream load fails with file-not-found; agent hallucinates content to compensate.
antipattern.root-cause: Picker uses its own naming heuristic instead of the closed list in the catalog.
antipattern.fix: Constrain the picker prompt with the explicit list of slugs from the matched section; reject any slug outside that list.

antipattern.description: Skipping the Glob verification step before reporting candidates.
antipattern.symptom: Reports contain resolved=false candidates that propagate to downstream load failure.
antipattern.root-cause: Picker treats catalog as authoritative; routing pipeline lacks a fail-closed verification step.
antipattern.fix: Make resolved=true mandatory; if Glob returns empty, add the slug to stale_rows and exclude from candidates.

antipattern.description: Pasting the full catalog into the downstream sub-agent prompt.
antipattern.symptom: Sub-agent prompt size balloons by 5-10k tokens; sub-agent re-picks slugs and ignores the resolved paths.
antipattern.root-cause: Misunderstanding of routing-step contract: the goal is to resolve, not to forward the menu.
antipattern.fix: Forward only the resolved candidates JSON; never the catalog rows.

antipattern.description: Using this catalog for semantic search ("how do I handle errors?").
antipattern.symptom: Picker scans names containing "error" and returns a broad set; many false positives.
antipattern.fix: For semantic queries, switch to `Grep -ril "...keyword..." skills/faion/knowledge` over actual content/*.xml files.
